data.body:
PLAN-REVIEW ROBUST FINDINGS (corpus 681/251/9792): (1) all would-block findings (priority>=0.70) are verifier-confirmed high-validity (mVal 0.97-1.00) + high-impact (0.78-0.83) => NOT nits/FPs; verifier is the adjudicator. (2) 0.70-0.75 is the reliability sweet spot: minimizes verdict-FLIP on identical code (5.1% all re-reviews / ~42% near-boundary, n small); BOTH lower (0.60=13.9%) and higher (0.80-0.90=86% near) flip MORE (rarer high-priority findings less reproducible). VINDICATES prior 0.70 choice. (3) The naive re-run of calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py demotes all 7 (rev_rr<0.6) but that's a FINDER-CHURN ARTIFACT (norm_id only ~41% stable on identical code; per-finding actioned signal unreliable). (4) Replay 97.9% faithful to recorded verdicts. (5) Trustworthy per-crit repro (n>=8): T8 85%(adv,n20), E2 62%, G6 50%(workhorse,127 blocks), T3 33%, T1 22%. T8 is best-reproducing but advisory; promoting doubles flip + agents ignore it. T5e/T4/G5 repro n=1 => can't act. CONCLUSION leaning: KEEP blocking-7@0.70; real limiter is finder non-determinism (needs N-sample corroboration, not threshold change). Block-rate ~19-24% of reviews.
CODE-REVIEW: running live evals (recall/false-accept/kappa per criterion) + 6 parallel live diff re-runs for priority distribution + adjudication of plan-review blocks. Historical: LLM blocks ~never, only DET secrets; nit vol 5/review.
